troubleshoot.title: Troubleshoot
troubleshoot.subtitle: Having issues with your PixelPlush game? Use the tools below to diagnose problems.
troubleshoot.checkUrlDesc: Paste the browser source URL from OBS to check for common issues.
troubleshoot.urlPlaceholder: Paste your OBS browser source URL here...
troubleshoot.commonIssuesDesc: Click an issue to see troubleshooting tips.
troubleshoot.generateReportDesc: Copy a summary of your diagnostics to share with us on Discord.
troubleshoot.stillStuck: Still stuck? Join our Discord or email us at support@pixelplush.dev
troubleshoot.setupChecklistDesc: First 3 items auto-check when you validate a URL. Click the rest to confirm manually.

nothing.title: Nothing shows up
nothing.tip1: Make sure the URL is correct — copy it fresh from the Games page.
nothing.tip2: Set OBS browser source dimensions to at least 1920×1080.
nothing.tip3: Disable "Shutdown source when not visible" in OBS.
nothing.tip4: Right-click the source → Refresh, or toggle visibility off/on.
nothing.tip5: Try the connection tester above to make sure servers are reachable.

chat.title: Game doesn't respond to chat
chat.tip1: Make sure the URL includes your channel name (e.g., ?channel=yourchannel).
chat.tip2: Wait 10–15 seconds after loading — the game needs a moment to connect.
chat.tip3: Try typing !play or !join in your chat.
chat.tip4: Tokens auto-refresh — if chat still doesn't work after a minute, regenerate the link.

channelpoints.title: Channel point rewards don't work
channelpoints.tip1: An OAuth token IS needed for channel points — make sure the URL has one.
channelpoints.tip2: Tokens auto-refresh — but if stale, regenerate the link from the Games page.
channelpoints.tip3: You must be a Twitch Affiliate or Partner for channel point rewards.
channelpoints.tip4: Check that you haven't created duplicate rewards with the same name.
channelpoints.tip5: Make sure the game overlay is loaded in OBS when testing.

freeze.title: Game freezes or stops
freeze.tip1: Enable "Refresh browser when scene becomes active" in OBS browser source settings.
freeze.tip2: Disable "Shutdown source when not visible" — this kills the game when switching scenes.
freeze.tip3: Check CPU/GPU usage — if OBS is struggling, the browser source may lag.
freeze.tip4: Right-click the source → Refresh to restart it.

flicker.title: Screen flickers
flicker.tip1: Update OBS to the latest version.
flicker.tip2: Use a single theme (don't rotate themes rapidly).
flicker.tip3: Toggle "Use custom frame rate" off in browser source settings.
flicker.tip4: Try disabling hardware acceleration in OBS: Settings → Advanced → uncheck "Enable Browser Source Hardware Acceleration".

confetti.title: Confetti/overlay doesn't appear
confetti.tip1: Confetti is passive — it doesn't show until triggered by a channel point redemption.
confetti.tip2: Test it by typing !confetti 10 in chat (if chat commands are enabled).
confetti.tip3: OAuth token is needed for channel point triggers — check the URL.
confetti.tip4: Make sure cpConfetti=true is in the URL for confetti functionality.

checklist.shutdown: "Shutdown source when not visible" is disabled
checklist.refresh: "Refresh browser when scene becomes active" is enabled

diag.httpsFail: Not using HTTPS. Some features may not work.
diag.channelFail: No "channel" parameter found. The game won't know which chat to connect to.
diag.oauthFail: No OAuth token. Channel Points and some features require a token. Games will auto-refresh tokens when possible.
diag.confettiInfo: This looks like a Confetti/overlay URL. Remember: it won't show anything until triggered.
diag.urlFormatFail: Invalid URL. Make sure you copied the full URL including https://

admin.title: Admin Panel
admin.subtitle: PixelPlush administration tools
admin.adminOnly: This page is only available to PixelPlush administrators.

giveaway.name: Giveaway Tool
giveaway.tagline: Fun way to do giveaways on Twitch
giveaway.description: Run giveaways in an easy and fun way for your viewers! Viewers type a command in chat to enter, and a random winner is picked with a fun animation.

weather.name: Stream Weather
weather.description: A weather-changing stream overlay that viewers can control using Channel Points. Rain, snow, thunderstorms — your viewers decide!

confetti.name: Pixel Confetti
confetti.tagline: Stream confetti overlay using Channel Points
confetti.description: Viewers send confetti across your stream using Channel Points! A fun visual celebration for subs, raids, or any hype moment.

plinko.name: Plinko Bounce
plinko.tagline: Plinko stream overlay game for Twitch
plinko.description: Drop a ball and watch it bounce through pegs to score points! Viewers compete for the highest score on the leaderboard.

parachute.name: Parachute Drop
parachute.tagline: Parachute stream overlay game for Twitch
parachute.description: Jump out of a plane, open your parachute, and try to land on the target! Viewers compete by typing commands in chat.

chatflakes.name: Chat Flakes
chatflakes.tagline: Chat messages turned into stream particles
chatflakes.description: Viewers add subtle particle effects across your screen just by typing in chat. A beautiful passive overlay!

hillroll.name: Hill Rolling Race
hillroll.tagline: Racing stream overlay game for Twitch
hillroll.description: Roll down a hill and race against other viewers! Dodge obstacles and try to reach the finish line first.

maze.name: Pixel Maze
maze.description: Navigate through a maze using chat commands! Great for BRB screens — keeps your viewers entertained during breaks.
